_meta.description: Complete closed-world edge confidence priors c(e) for the Koi CandidateGraph. EVERY admissible edge (all X->V and all V->Y) has an LLM-assigned confidence. The LLM never adds edges - it only updates these priors via the four-quadrant/Beta loop - so no edge is omitted: an absent edge would be impossible, not undiscovered.
_meta.format: Nested matrix. x_to_v[V][X] = c(X->V) for every X. v_to_y[Y][V] = c(V->Y) for every V. edge_id is f'{src}->{dst}'. src_type/dst_type are implied by the section (X->V or V->Y).

confidence_scale.0.20-0.44: unlikely but a conceivable indirect pathway
confidence_scale.0.05-0.19: no known causal pathway; present only for closed-world discoverability

_meta.method: Curated edges carry specific domain values. Non-curated edges carry a category-based prior reflecting whether that class of X plausibly drives that V/Y; placement/identity labels (cloud, region, market, instance_type, runtime_image, gpu_watts) get the lowest priors because their only effect on runtime V/Y is mediated by hardware/workload features that are themselves nodes.
_meta.beta_seeding: alpha = c*S, beta = (1-c)*S. seed_candidate_graph uses S=16 for c>=0.92 (sticky physics), S=4 for 0.45<=c<0.92 (movable), S=2 for c<0.45 (evidence dominates fast).
_meta.beta_prior_format: Each edge leaf is {c, alpha, beta, strength}. c is the curated prior mean; alpha=c*strength and beta=(1-c)*strength are the Beta prior parameters used by Koi.
_meta.confidence_scale_note: Confidence values are no longer updated directly. Runtime updates modify alpha/beta; confidence is derived as alpha/(alpha+beta), and EIG uncertainty uses Beta posterior variance.
